contribution.title: Event Horizon Telescope founding director
contribution.description: Led the EHT collaboration that produced the first horizon-scale images of M87* (2019) and Sgr A* (2022).
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Provides the observational_constraint reference for the horizon-scale ring (M87*: 42 +/- 3 microarcseconds; Sgr A*: 51.8 +/- 2.3 microarcseconds).

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Push interferometric baselines to Earth-diameter scale; use closure quantities (closure phase, closure amplitude) that are robust to station errors.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What is the closure phase of the source brightness distribution?
Is the ring asymmetric, and what does that tell us about the spin axis?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Imaging at the diffraction limit is sensitive to prior assumptions; EHT collaboration carefully cross-validates with multiple algorithms.

reference.citation: Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration (2019). First M87 Event Horizon Telescope results. I-VI. Astrophys. J. Lett. 875.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Using horizon-scale imaging data as a constraint or validation target.
how_to_apply.cautions: Reported uncertainties are non-Gaussian; do not naively convolve them as if they were.
